title: Assemble Perentie chassis components, part 1: shock mounts, bushes, steering box
note: Torque and fastener values are transcribed from the SME's narration and MUST be verified against spec before use. Part numbers were never spoken. Suppliers confirmed by Dave: Wurth (shock-mount bolts), British Auto Parts (imperial bracket + bush bolts), SuperPro (bushes). This is part 1 of a multi-part assembly doc (SME says 'the rest I'll do in another process document').

steps.title: Fit the shock absorber mounts
steps.fastener: 3 x M12 x 110 mm, brand new
steps.body: Three bolts per shock absorber mount. Use brand-new M12 x 110 mm fasteners from the shelf. Torque to 90 Nm.

steps.title: Bolt the front brackets (watch orientation)
steps.body: These are imperial bolts. The front two on each bracket are longer than the rear ones and go in with the nut facing down. The bolts on the front of the bracket have the nuts facing up. Torque all to 81 Nm.

steps.title: Fit the bush bolts (both sides)
steps.body: Bush bolts, torqued to 90 Nm. Same on the other side.

steps.title: Press in the SuperPro bushes
steps.body: The bushes come from SuperPro, same as the others. They are a press fit and just pop in.

steps.title: Mount the steering box
steps.body: The steering box takes four M12 bolts through into the mount. Off-the-shelf, grade 10.9. Torque to 81 Nm.

steps.title: Refit the original imperial bolts below the steering box
steps.body: The bolts under here also torque to 81 Nm. Reuse the original bolts. Metric fasteners do not fit through this gap, so these stay the original imperial size.

steps.title: Fit the long chassis through-bolts
steps.body: Two long bolts that run all the way through the chassis. Torque to 90 Nm.

steps.title: Fit the shock tower top fasteners (do not over-tighten)
steps.body: The fasteners around the tops of the shock absorber towers are quite fragile, so do not over-tighten. Roughly 40 Nm, but the SME explicitly said to check the spec on this one.
